llmTip: CRITICAL: When searching emails, the $search parameter value MUST be wrapped in double quotes. Format: $search="your search query here". Use KQL (Keyword Query Language) syntax to search specific properties: 'from:', 'subject:', 'body:', 'to:', 'cc:', 'bcc:', 'attachment:', 'hasAttachments:', 'importance:', 'received:', 'sent:'. Examples: $search="from:john@example.com" | $search="subject:meeting AND hasAttachments:true" | $search="body:urgent AND received>=2024-01-01" | $search="from:john AND importance:high". Remember: ALWAYS wrap the entire search expression in double quotes! Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/search-query-parameter

CRITICAL: When searching emails, the $search parameter value MUST be wrapped in double quotes. Format: $search="your search query here". Use KQL (Keyword Query Language) syntax to search specific properties: 'from:', 'subject:', 'body:', 'to:', 'cc:', 'bcc:', 'attachment:', 'hasAttachments:', 'importance:', 'received:', 'sent:'. Examples: $search="from:john@example.com" | $search="subject:meeting AND hasAttachments:true" | $search="body:urgent AND received>=2024-01-01" | $search="from:alice AND importance:high". Remember: ALWAYS wrap the entire search expression in double quotes! Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/search-query-parameter
CRITICAL: Do not try to guess the email address of the recipients. Use the list-users tool to find the email address of the recipients.

CRITICAL: This request requires the ConsistencyLevel header set to eventual. When searching users, the $search parameter value MUST be wrapped in double quotes. Format: $search="your search query here". Use KQL (Keyword Query Language) syntax to search specific properties: 'displayName:'. Examples: $search="displayName:john" | $search="displayName:john" OR "displayName:jane". Remember: ALWAYS wrap the entire search expression in double quotes and set the ConsistencyLevel header to eventual! Reference: https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/graph/search-query-parameter

Forward an email preserving full HTML formatting and attachments. The 'comment' field adds text above the forwarded content. toRecipients is required. Do NOT reconstruct the email manually - this endpoint handles everything server-side.

Reply to an email and send immediately. Use the 'comment' field with HTML for your reply — it supports full HTML rendering AND automatically appends the thread. Do NOT use Message.body (wipes thread). Do NOT reconstruct the email manually.

Reply-all to an email and send immediately. Use the 'comment' field with HTML for your reply — it supports full HTML rendering AND automatically appends the thread. Do NOT use Message.body (wipes thread). Do NOT reconstruct the email manually.

Create a forward draft (does not send). PREFERRED APPROACH: Use the 'Comment' field with HTML — it supports full HTML rendering AND automatically appends the original message thread. Do NOT use Message.body, as it replaces the entire body and wipes the thread. Example: { "Comment": "

Your formatted note here

", "Message": { "toRecipients": [{"emailAddress":{"address":"user@example.com"}}] } }.

Create a reply draft (does not send). PREFERRED APPROACH: Use the 'Comment' field with HTML — it supports full HTML rendering (bold, italics, links all work) AND automatically appends the full original thread with Outlook separator. Do NOT use Message.body, as it replaces the entire body and wipes the thread. Example: { "Comment": "

Your formatted reply here

" }.

Create a reply-all draft (does not send). PREFERRED APPROACH: Use the 'Comment' field with HTML — it supports full HTML rendering (bold, italics, links all work) AND automatically appends the full original thread with Outlook separator. Do NOT use Message.body, as it replaces the entire body and wipes the thread. Example: { "Comment": "

Your formatted reply here

" }.
CRITICAL: Do not try to guess the email address of the recipients. Use the list-users tool to find the email address of the recipients.
CRITICAL: Do not try to guess the email address of the recipients. Use the list-users tool to find the email address of the recipients.
CRITICAL: Do not try to guess the email address of the recipients. Use the list-users tool to find the email address of the recipients.
CRITICAL: Do not try to guess the email address of the recipients. Use the list-users tool to find the email address of the recipients.

Returns expanded recurring event instances (not just seriesMaster) within a date range for the default calendar. Requires startDateTime and endDateTime query parameters in ISO 8601 format (e.g., 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z). Use get-specific-calendar-view if you need a non-default calendar.

Returns expanded recurring event instances (not just seriesMaster) within a date range for a specific calendar. Requires startDateTime and endDateTime query parameters in ISO 8601 format (e.g., 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z). Each instance includes seriesMasterId and type (occurrence/exception) fields for recurring event linkage. Use fetchAllPages=true to retrieve all results when there are many events.

Expand a recurring event into individual instances within a date range. Requires startDateTime and endDateTime query parameters in ISO 8601 format (e.g., 2024-01-01T00:00:00Z). Use this to see all occurrences of a recurring event.
Copy a message to a folder. Body requires destinationId (the target folder ID).
Returns user mailbox settings including automaticRepliesSetting (out of office), language, timeZone, dateFormat, timeFormat, and delegateMeetingMessageDeliveryOptions.

Update mailbox settings including automaticRepliesSetting (out of office), language, timeZone, etc. For OOF: set status to 'alwaysEnabled', 'scheduled', or 'disabled' with internalReplyMessage and externalReplyMessage.
Accept a meeting invitation. Body: { comment: string (optional), sendResponse: boolean }. Set sendResponse to true to notify the organizer.
Decline a meeting invitation. Body: { comment: string (optional), sendResponse: boolean, proposedNewTime: { start, end } (optional) }.
Tentatively accept a meeting invitation. Body: { comment: string (optional), sendResponse: boolean, proposedNewTime: { start, end } (optional) }.
Cancel an event you organized. Sends cancellation to all attendees. Body: { comment: string (optional) }.
Forward a calendar event to new recipients. Body: { toRecipients: [{ emailAddress: { address, name } }], comment: string (optional) }.

Get free/busy schedule for multiple users. Body: { schedules: ['user@example.com'], startTime: { dateTime, timeZone }, endTime: { dateTime, timeZone }, availabilityViewInterval: 30 }. Returns availabilityView string where 0=free, 1=tentative, 2=busy, 3=oof, 4=working-elsewhere.
Get the current user's default OneDrive. Returns drive metadata including id, name, quota info. Use the drive id with other drive endpoints.
Get metadata for a specific file or folder by its ID. Returns name, size, lastModifiedDateTime, webUrl, and parent info.
Update a drive item's metadata. Can rename items by setting { name: 'new-name.docx' } or move items by changing parentReference.
Create a new folder. Body: { name: 'New Folder', folder: {}, '@microsoft.graph.conflictBehavior': 'rename' }. The parent is specified by the driveItem-id in the path.
Copy a file or folder. Body: { parentReference: { driveId, id }, name: 'copy-name' (optional) }. Returns a Location header with the monitor URL for async copy status.
Create a sharing link for a file or folder. Body: { type: 'view'|'edit'|'embed', scope: 'anonymous'|'organization', password: string (optional), expirationDateTime: string (optional) }.
Search for files by name or content within a drive. The q parameter is the search query string. Returns matching driveItems.
List files the user has recently accessed. Returns the most recently used files across the drive.
List files shared with the current user from other users or drives.
Update cell values in a range. Body: { values: [[row1col1, row1col2], [row2col1, row2col2]] }. The values array must match the dimensions of the range.
Create a table from a range. Body: { address: 'Sheet1!A1:D10', hasHeaders: true }.
Add a row to a table. Body: { values: [[col1val, col2val, col3val]], index: null }. Set index to null to append at the end.
Create a persistent workbook session for batching multiple operations. Body: { persistChanges: true }. Returns a session ID to use in the workbook-session-id header for subsequent requests.
Link an external resource to a task. Body: { webUrl: 'https://...', applicationName: 'App Name', displayName: 'Resource Title' }.
List all Planner plans assigned to the current user.
Get detailed plan info including category descriptions (labels). The categoryDescriptions object maps category1-category25 to label names.
Update plan details like category descriptions. Requires If-Match header with the ETag. Use includeHeaders=true on the GET to capture the ETag.
Create a bucket in a plan. Body: { name: 'Bucket Name', planId: '...', orderHint: ' !' }.
Requires If-Match header with the ETag. Use includeHeaders=true on the GET to capture the ETag.
Requires If-Match header with the ETag.
Requires If-Match header with the ETag. Use includeHeaders=true on get-planner-task to capture the ETag.
Get task details including description, checklist items, references/links, and previewType.
List all sections across all notebooks. Use list-onenote-notebook-sections to list sections within a specific notebook.
Update page content using PATCH commands. Body: [ { target: '#element-id', action: 'append|replace|delete|insert|prepend', content: '<html>' } ]. Target uses CSS selectors.
Copy a page to a different section. Body: { id: 'target-section-id' }.
Copy a section to a different notebook. Body: { id: 'target-notebook-id' }.

Create a new chat. For 1:1: { chatType: 'oneOnOne', members: [{ '@odata.type': '#microsoft.graph.aadUserConversationMember', roles: ['owner'], 'user@odata.bind': 'https://graph.microsoft.com/v1.0/users/USER-ID' }, ...] }. For group: chatType: 'group' with a topic.

Create a Teams online meeting. Body: { subject: 'Meeting Title', startDateTime: '2024-01-01T10:00:00Z', endDateTime: '2024-01-01T11:00:00Z', participants: { attendees: [{ upn: 'user@example.com' }] } }. Returns joinWebUrl for meeting link.
List Microsoft 365 groups and security groups. Use $filter=groupTypes/any(c:c eq 'Unified') to get only Microsoft 365 groups. Use $search="displayName:keyword" with ConsistencyLevel=eventual header.
Get the Team associated with a Microsoft 365 group. Not all groups have Teams - this returns 404 if no Team exists.
List Planner plans owned by a group. This is the primary way to discover plans - plans are always owned by a group.
Create a list item. Body: { fields: { Title: 'value', ColumnName: 'value' } }. Field names must match the internal column names of the list.
Update a list item's field values. Body: { fields: { ColumnName: 'new value' } }.
Get all field values for a specific list item. Returns the actual column values.

List people relevant to the current user, ordered by relevance (communication, collaboration, and business relationships). Supports $search and $filter. Great for finding colleagues the user frequently interacts with.
Returns documents trending around the user. Shows files gaining attention based on the user's closest network of people.
Returns documents the user has recently viewed or modified. Useful for finding recently worked-on files.
Returns documents shared with the user via email, Teams, or OneDrive sharing.
List all groups, directory roles, and administrative units the current user is a member of.
Get the current user's presence status. Returns availability (Available, Busy, DoNotDisturb, etc.) and activity (InACall, InAMeeting, Presenting, etc.).
Get presence for multiple users. Use $filter with id for specific users. More efficient than individual calls.

Set the current user's presence. Body: { sessionId: 'app-guid', availability: 'Available|Busy|DoNotDisturb|BeRightBack|Away|Offline', activity: 'Available|InACall|InAMeeting|...', expirationDuration: 'PT1H' }.

Set a status message displayed in Teams. Body: { statusMessage: { message: { content: 'Working from home', contentType: 'text' }, expiryDateTime: { dateTime: '2024-12-31T23:59:59Z', timeZone: 'UTC' } } }.
List active webhook subscriptions for the current app.

Create a webhook subscription. Body: { changeType: 'created,updated,deleted', notificationUrl: 'https://...', resource: 'me/messages' or 'me/events' or 'me/contacts', expirationDateTime: '2024-12-31T00:00:00Z', clientState: 'secretValue' }. The scope needed depends on the resource being subscribed to.
Renew or update a subscription. Typically used to extend expirationDateTime before it expires.
Get a specific user by their ID or userPrincipalName. Example: /users/john@contoso.com or /users/{guid}.
Get the user's manager. Returns a directoryObject (typically a user). Use $select=displayName,mail,jobTitle to get useful fields.
Get the user's profile photo as binary data.
List groups, directory roles, and administrative units the user is a member of.
List all conference rooms in the organization. Returns room details including displayName, emailAddress, capacity, building, floor.
List room lists (groups of rooms, typically by building). Each room list has an emailAddress that can be used to find rooms in that list.
